text: I think there are a few reasons for this…one superhero fatigue aka bad movie fatigue, two Marvel and DC have gone all through their main characters, so know unless you’re a massive Thunderbolts fan nobody is really going to care, and finally the studios should of paced their projects out over the years instead of months.

replies.author: @Handle-h5x5n
replies.text:
Yep, fatigue. Over saturation. Woke Agenda in a 80% Male market.

 Way over scheduling. Everyone getting a show or film that no one cared about. Even the hardcores.

Streaming wars. Covid or govt shutting down theaters. Writers strike. AI.

D+ crap for 200mil$ a pop. Taking years to complete. Marvels rushed cg and forced humor on top of that.

Marvel switching directors time and again for its major projects.

Marvel rescripts and reshoots, rinse/repeat. Marvel changing scripts to be China friendly.

No Avengers and no real continuity to such in phase 4 & 5. So many credit scenes that led to nowhere.

Marvel films each costing like 300-400$mil before PR. 

Box office 50%+ going to theaters.

Watching Marvel now feels like work instead of entertainment.

replies.text: @doge-palno we hate phase 4 and 5 keep up boot licker

replies.author: @Hrjvdyunvhb
replies.text: @doge-palbum

replies.author: @Handle-h5x5n
replies.text:
Fatigue. Over saturation. Woke Agenda in 80% Male markets. No legacy characters. Destroying classic villians. Streaming wars. Covid shut downs. Writers strikes. AI. D+ crap at 200$mil a pop. Taking many years to complete or rushed projects. Re shoots, rescripts and changing directors on so many projects. Bad writing, forced humor, rushed cg. No Avengers films or no continuity in Phase 4 thru 5. Many credit scenes that went nowhere. Marvel making films at 300-400$mil before PR budget. Where theaters get 50+% of the box office. Changing scripts, stories to he China friendly. 

It's death by a thousand cuts and Disney is the blade.

replies.author: @Handle-h5x5n

And look at all the animated films like Inside Out 2, Despicable Me 4, Kung-fu Panda 4, Minecraft etc making bank last few years.

Marvel & other IPs need to throw out their board room, woke, shock value over quality formula.

author: @themagicalblingbling
text: I would probably still be willing to catch up with the MCU had they only released films, be they good or bad, if a project further down the line was opened to rave reviews. But the fact that I'm expected to sit through tens of series just to be able to know the full story? That just guarantees that I'm never returning to the MCU, I'm not putting in that time and effort, knowing that even three films a year during phase three already started to feel like overkill to me.

replies.author: @Mouse_Metal
replies.text:
Exactly. I don´t know why everyone in Hollyweird thinks blackmailing people into watching some trash-tier shows just to understand the main story is a good strategy. They are doing the same with Star Wars and their fanfic-tier shows about Dave Failoni´s pet Mary Sues nobody except of him cares about. Somehow they believe it´s a good move to connect everything to everything else, while they most popular stuff are the standalones - Rogue One and the first season of Mando. 
People want to watch stuff about particular characters they care about and avoid stuff about characters they can´t stand. It´s impossible if everything is connected and the insufferable Mary Sues are shoehorned into everything. Good luck with making the Mando and Grogu movie (in the name only, that´s for sure) "very connected to Ahsoka show".

author: @goji3755
text:
This sort of thinking led to Ultimatum some 15 years ago - an event comic that was supposed to trim the Ultimate universe's relevant history and create a jumping-on point for new readers. It was one of the most unnecessarily gruesome, hateful stories Marvel ever printed, in which they massacred the majority of the Ultimate timeline's major characters and as a result, lost far more readers than they gained, throwing the Ultimate line into a death spiral that wound up getting it cancelled a few years later.

One wonders if we might see the MCU make a similar mistake in the coming years? Attempt to wipe away its bloated cast and undesirable history to gain new viewership, only to strangle itself.

author: @gurugamer3196
text: Following the insults directed toward their fanbase, one must consider what outcome was anticipated. A sense of apathy has manifested, resulting in a lack of concern from the audience. Disney has effectively undermined two franchises that previously generated significant revenue, rendering them incapable of incentivizing fans to return. I exemplify the notion of a dedicated fan; however, upon learning about "Thunderbolts," I found myself devoid of interest in any project associated with Disney. It would make no difference if it turned out to be the most acclaimed film of the year. Disney appears to mistakenly believe that fans have fleeting memories, yet the reality is quite the opposite; fans remember everything.

author: @Bad_Llama
text: The point of interlocking storylines wasn't to make fans watch all the movies.  It was to give the people who go to the other movies a thrill of knowing more about the deeper lore because they took the time.  Make great movies and everyone gets that thrill, but you still don't make it so you can't enjoy the movie without seeing the previous work.  It you make all movies and TV mandatory to even know who is who and what is going on and you lose the customer.  Make crap and you lose the customer.  Do both and you lose all the customers past, present, and future.

author: @Eric-xk3rt
text:
My problem is: Thunderbolts is an excellent (comics) concept, that consistently suffered from lackluster execution. It's also essentially a 'deep-cut'.

I don't reserve much affection for the MCU at this point. They've been careless to the degree that their 'competence' resembles spite. Even so, a simple role call of their current roster immediately reveals that they don't have the necessary pieces on the game board to attempt a Thunderbolts adaptation.

Another Whodunnit concluding in the MCU having shot itself in the foot, for no apparent reason. This apparently is their "Endgame", and they are poised for a repeat with Doomsday.
